html:
Exclusive to:

“Can be planted on Sea Stacks.”

-

The Sea Sprout Starter is an Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in Return of Them.

When a Sea Weed is killed, a single Sea Sprout Starter is dropped. It can then be placed on a Sea Stack to grow back into a Sea Sprout.

Trivia

  • The Sea Sprout Starter was introduced in the Troubled Waters update.

“They seem to grow best on oceanic rocks.”
–Wilson
“Do I really want to grow more of these things?”
–Willow
“It needs rock for the growing.”
–Wolfgang
“Even if I plant it, the fickle thing may turn against me.”
–Wendy
“PERHAPS YOU COULD BE USEFUL TO ME”
–WX-78
“Perhaps I'll get back into gardening.”
–Wickerbottom
“Where will you cause the least amount of damage...”
–Woodie
“Why would I want these to spread?”
–Maxwell
“Fear nöt, I shall return yöu tö the sea in time.”
–Wigfrid
“Where should we put you?”
–Webber
“Where shall I start planting my sea garden?”
–Warly
“Find nice spot for friend”
–Wormwood
“Let's find a good rock to plant you on.”
–Winona
“A bouncing baby bulb.”
–Wortox
“Maybe we just throw it into the big pond, florp.”
–Wurt
“Don't worry, we'll find a nice rock to plant you on.”
–Walter
“If I come across a nice rock, I'll leave it there.”
–Wanda
“Can be planted on Sea Stacks.”

The Sea Sprout Starter is an Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in Return of Them.
When a Sea Weed is killed, a single Sea Sprout Starter is dropped. It can then be placed on a Sea Stack to grow back into a Sea Sprout.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Sea Sprout Starter was introduced in the Troubled Waters update.

The '''Sea Sprout Starter''' is an [[Item]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''.

When a [[Sea Weed]] is killed, a single Sea Sprout Starter is dropped. It can then be placed on a [[Sea Stack]] to grow back into a [[Sea Sprout]].

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}}Trivia ==
* The Sea Sprout Starter was introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Troubled Waters|Troubled Waters]]'' update.
